chapter_title.ar: بَابُ صَيْدِ الْحَرَمِ وَ مَا تَجِبُ فِيهِ الْكَفَّارَةُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 21 – Hunt of the Al-Harram and what expiation is Obligated with regards to it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/86)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/86)
text: مَنْ أَصَابَ طَيْراً فِي الْحَرَمِ وَ هُوَ مُحِلٌّ فَعَلَيْهِ الْقِيمَةُ وَ الْقِيمَةُ دِرْهَمٌ يَشْتَرِي بِهِ عَلَفاً لِحَمَامِ الْحَرَمِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Safwan Bin Yahya,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who hits a bird inside the Al-Harram and he is free (not in Ihram), so upon him is the price, and the price is one Dirham. He buys feed with it for the doves of the Al-Harram’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Safwan ibn Yahya who has said the following: “Abu al-Hassan al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one hurts a bird in al-Haram (the sacred area) when one is not in the state of ’Ihram, he owes its price. This price is one dirham with which he buys feed for the pigeons of al-Haram (the sacred area).”’
